steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains an em dash after "light" and before "reshuffled" — so the phrase "the cellular organs that generate energy from light" is an appositive set off by em dashes on both sides. The blank must open that em-dash pair, meaning the word "chloroplasts" needs an em dash immediately after it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — chloroplasts,: a comma does not match the em dash that closes the appositive; mismatched punctuation is a Standard English error. ✗
  • Choice B — chloroplasts—: an em dash opens the appositive, matching the closing em dash before "reshuffled" — both sides are properly set off. ✓
  • Choice C — chloroplasts: no punctuation leaves "the cellular organs..." running directly into the subject with no separator, creating ambiguity. ✗
  • Choice D — chloroplasts;: a semicolon signals the end of an independent clause, but what follows is not an independent clause — it is an appositive phrase. ✗
B is correct.
